prompts.abstract: The object is a rectangular block with a flat top and bottom. It has a uniform thickness throughout its length and width. The edges are straight and sharp.
prompts.description: The object is a rectangular block with a flat top and bottom. It has a uniform thickness throughout its length and width. The edges are straight and sharp.
prompts.beginner: The design involves creating a slender rectangular block with a square base. The block stands tall, with its height being much greater than its width and length.
prompts.intermediate: Start by creating a new coordinate system with specific rotations and translations. Draw a square on a new face, then scale this square to the desired size. Transform the scaled square into a three-dimensional object by applying specific rotations and translations. Extrude the transformed square along its normal direction to create a rectangular block. The block will have a square base with equal sides and a height that is significantly longer than the base dimensions. The final step involves creating a solid body from the extruded shape. The dimensions of the block are such that the height is much greater than the width and length, forming a slender rectangular prism.
prompts.expert: Create a new coordinate system by setting the Euler angles to zero, zero, and negative ninety degrees. Set the translation vector to zero, zero point seven five, and zero. Next, draw a two-dimensional sketch on a new face. Begin by drawing a closed loop made up of four lines. The first line starts at the origin and ends at zero point one three five, zero. The second line starts at zero point one three five, zero and ends at zero point one three five, zero point one three five. The third line starts at zero point one three five, zero point one three five and ends at zero, zero point one three five. The fourth line completes the loop by starting at zero, zero point one three five and ending at the origin. Apply a scaling factor of zero point one three five to the entire two-dimensional sketch. Transform the scaled two-dimensional sketch into three dimensions by rotating it using the Euler angles zero, zero, and negative ninety degrees, followed by translating it using the vector zero, zero point seven five, and zero. Extrude the transformed two-dimensional sketch along the normal direction by zero point seven five units, ensuring no extrusion occurs in the opposite direction. Finally, create a new solid body from the extruded sketch. The resulting rectangular block will have a length of zero point one three five units, a width of zero point one three five units, and a height of zero point seven five units.
prompts.all_level_data:
<level1>
Create a new coordinate system by setting the Euler angles to zero, zero, and negative ninety degrees. Set the translation vector to zero, zero point seven five, and zero. Next, draw a two-dimensional sketch on a new face. Begin by drawing a closed loop made up of four lines. The first line starts at the origin and ends at zero point one three five, zero. The second line starts at zero point one three five, zero and ends at zero point one three five, zero point one three five. The third line starts at zero point one three five, zero point one three five and ends at zero, zero point one three five. The fourth line completes the loop by starting at zero, zero point one three five and ending at the origin. Apply a scaling factor of zero point one three five to the entire two-dimensional sketch. Transform the scaled two-dimensional sketch into three dimensions by rotating it using the Euler angles zero, zero, and negative ninety degrees, followed by translating it using the vector zero, zero point seven five, and zero. Extrude the transformed two-dimensional sketch along the normal direction by zero point seven five units, ensuring no extrusion occurs in the opposite direction. Finally, create a new solid body from the extruded sketch. The resulting rectangular block will have a length of zero point one three five units, a width of zero point one three five units, and a height of zero point seven five units.
</level1>

<level2>
Start by creating a new coordinate system with specific rotations and translations. Draw a square on a new face, then scale this square to the desired size. Transform the scaled square into a three-dimensional object by applying specific rotations and translations. Extrude the transformed square along its normal direction to create a rectangular block. The block will have a square base with equal sides and a height that is significantly longer than the base dimensions. The final step involves creating a solid body from the extruded shape. The dimensions of the block are such that the height is much greater than the width and length, forming a slender rectangular prism.
</level2>

<level3>
The design involves creating a slender rectangular block with a square base. The block stands tall, with its height being much greater than its width and length.
</level3>
prompts.nli_data:
<part_1>
### Construct a Rectangular Block

#### Step 1: Create a New Coordinate System
- Set the Euler angles to [0.0, 0.0, -90.0].
- Set the translation vector to [0.0, 0.75, 0.0].

#### Step 2: Draw a 2D Sketch
- Create a new face.
- Draw a closed loop consisting of four lines:
  - **Line 1**: Start at (0.0, 0.0) and end at (0.135, 0.0).
  - **Line 2**: Start at (0.135, 0.0) and end at (0.135, 0.135).
  - **Line 3**: Start at (0.135, 0.135) and end at (0.0, 0.135).
  - **Line 4**: Start at (0.0, 0.135) and end at (0.0, 0.0).

#### Step 3: Scale the 2D Sketch
- Apply a scaling factor of 0.135 to the 2D sketch.

#### Step 4: Transform the Scaled 2D Sketch into 3D
- Rotate the 2D sketch using the Euler angles [0.0, 0.0, -90.0].
- Translate the 2D sketch using the translation vector [0.0, 0.75, 0.0].

#### Step 5: Extrude the 2D Sketch to Generate the 3D Model
- Extrude the 2D sketch along the normal direction by 0.75 units.
- Do not extrude in the opposite direction.

#### Step 6: Integrate the Part
- Create a new solid body from the extruded sketch.

#### Dimensions
- Length: 0.135 units
- Width: 0.135 units
- Height: 0.75 units

</part_1>
